formations_blocks: Austria early pressing/settling after long balls from defence
Jordan compact counterattacking setup, sitting back and pouncing on loose balls
Jordan playing out from the back at times before transitioning quickly
Austria flooding the penalty area when Jordan attack the left
adjustments: Austria began with long balls out of defence and a quick throw-in strategy
Jordan shifted from defending deep to brief spells of possession in the attacking half
Austria’s defensive recovery saw them crowd the box to isolate Musa Al-Taamari
key_matchups: Musa Al-Taamari vs Austria’s recovering defence
Konrad Laimer vs Jordan’s defensive line in the penalty area
David Alaba operating from the back and stepping into midfield
phases.summary: The opening quarter-hour was defined by Austria trying to establish Rangnick-style pressure and territorial control, largely through long balls, quick restarts and David Alaba’s deep orchestration. Jordan were not overawed: they defended compactly, broke quickly through Musa Al-Taamari, and also showed they could play out from the back and progress down the left before Austria’s numbers recovered in the box.

formations_blocks: Austria building attacks through central combinations
adjustments: Austria using Saša Kalajdžić as the target man
Austria increasing pressure with attacking-third throw-ins and second-ball attacks
Jordan staying compact and waiting to break quickly
Austria progressing through one-touch passing around the top of the box
key_matchups: Saša Kalajdžić vs Jordan centre-backs
Romano Schmid vs Jordan defensive line
Xaver Schlager running through Jordan's midfield
Odeh Al-Fakhouri vs Austria's defensive line
Ali Olwan vs Austria centre-backs
Konrad Laimer vs Jordan defender tugging him back
phases.summary: Austria took control of midfield and spent much of the phase probing Jordan’s deeper defensive block, with Kalajdžić acting as the central reference point and Schmid’s well-worked finish giving them the lead. Jordan threatened on the counter and nearly equalised immediately after the goal, but Austria kept regaining territory and pressure through quick restarts, second balls, and central progression.

formations_blocks: Jordan sitting back in a counter-attacking setup
adjustments: Marko Arnautović ready to enter at half-time
key_matchups: Austria midfield vs Jordan's deep defensive block
Saša Kalajdžić vs Jordan central defenders
Musa Al-Taamari as Jordan's counter-attacking outlet
phases.summary: Austria went in 1-0 up after dictating more of the ball and trying to play through midfield into Saša Kalajdžić, while Jordan were content to absorb pressure and break quickly through Musa Al-Taamari. The match was fairly even in chances, with Jordan actually matching or exceeding Austria in several shot metrics, but Romano Schmid’s long-range strike separated the sides at the break. Austria’s half-time adjustment was already visible with Marko Arnautović preparing to replace Kalajdžić, suggesting a change in their attacking reference point.

formations_blocks: Jordan chasing the game with more aggressive attacking intent
Austria slowing the tempo and circulating possession through midfield and the back line after taking the lead
Jordan repeatedly finding space down the right side in wide attacking moves
adjustments: Jordan resumed with the same XI and immediately set out to chase the game
Austria attempted to control momentum by slowing the game down through Schlager and Seiwald
Austria made a triple change on 59' with Danso, Chukwuemeka and Wanner coming on; Alaba, Schlager and Mwene went off
Austria took time to settle after the substitutions, with Chukwuemeka injecting energy
key_matchups: Ehsan Haddad vs Austria's defensive cover on the right
Odeh Al-Fakhouri vs Austria's right-side defensive response
Carney Chukwuemeka vs Jordan's penalty-area resistance
Ali Olwan vs Austria's exposed defensive space
phases.summary: Jordan began the second half with clear attacking intent, repeatedly using the right flank and eventually exploiting Austria’s loose defensive spacing for the equaliser through Ali Olwan. Austria responded by slowing the match, then reshaped with a triple substitution before regaining control enough to restore the lead through Arnautović.

formations_blocks: Austria’s press slowing the flow of the game
adjustments: Jordan replaced Abdallah Nasib and captain Ehsan Haddad with Saed Al-Rosan and attacker Mahmoud Al-Mardi
Austria replaced goalscorer Romano Schmid with Patrick Wimmer
Jordan introduced Mohammad Al-Dawoud and Ali Azaizeh for Musa Al-Taamari and Odeh Al-Fakhouri
Austria had only Marko Arnautović left high as they defended deep late on
key_matchups: Marko Arnautović vs Yazeed Abulaila
Noor Al-Rawabdeh under pressure from Austria’s press
Yazan Al-Arab dealing with the aerial/defensive action before his own-goal
Jordan’s forward surges vs Austria’s deep defensive line
phases.summary: Austria regained the lead via an own goal and then shifted into a more conservative, deep-defending posture, using pressure at the right moments to slow Jordan’s momentum. Jordan responded with multiple attacking substitutions and greater urgency, but their risk-taking opened space for Austria to counter, with Arnautović nearly sealing it before finally scoring in stoppage time.
